characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A well-meaning, civic-minded lawyer in his 30s, Nick is a husband, father, and son to the Darling family attorney, Devlin "Dutch" George and reluctantly takes over the role after his father's sudden death, having now to balance his moral beliefs, his loyalty to the Darlings, and the toll the job takes on his personal life. The Ace: Nick is consistently portrayed as highly competent at lawyering, crisis management, and problem-solving, with the rest of the cast being either envious or in awe of his talent. Amateur Sleuth: He wasn't keen on working for the Darlings after a terrible first day, but he decides not to quit when he finds out maybe one day is responsible for his father's death, and keeps the job so he can use his position to investigate them himself. Beleaguered Assistant: Nick's constantly overwhelmed by being the Darlings' problem solver and having to deal with endless crises and petty disputes. Cannot Tell a Lie: Nick's very uncomfortable with lying and disappoints Tripp several times by refusing to do it for the sake of the Darlings.

characters.name: Nick George
characters.description: A well-meaning, civic-minded lawyer in his 30s, Nick is a husband, father, and son to the Darling family attorney, Devlin "Dutch" George and reluctantly takes over the role after his father's sudden death, having now to balance his moral beliefs, his loyalty to the Darlings, and the toll the job takes on his personal life. The Ace: Nick is consistently portrayed as highly competent at lawyering, crisis management, and problem-solving, with the rest of the cast being either envious or in awe of his talent. Amateur Sleuth: He wasn't keen on working for the Darlings after a terrible first day, but he decides not to quit when he finds out maybe one day is responsible for his father's death, and keeps the job so he can use his position to investigate them himself. Beleaguered Assistant: Nick's constantly overwhelmed by being the Darlings' problem solver and having to deal with endless crises and petty disputes. Cannot Tell a Lie: Nick's very uncomfortable with lying and disappoints Tripp several times by refusing to do it for the sake of the Darlings.

characters.name: Lisa George
characters.description: Nick’s wife (later ex-wife), an art dealer who initially tries to keep her distance from the Darling family. Over time, her life becomes increasingly entangled with the Darlings’ wealth and influence, contributing to the breakdown of her marriage to Nick. Betty and Veronica: Lisa is positioned as the Betty in theLove Trianglewith her, Nick and Karen, in contrast to Karen, Nick's glamorousNew Old Flame. Buxom Beauty Standard: When Jeremy is pretending to be aStarving Artistand needs to do a painting of his busty girlfriend, he has the idea of Lisa modeling for him as practice, since they have a similar body, and makes a cupping gesture to make clear what he means. Clingy Jealous Girl:For good reasonthanks to Karen's blatant attempts to seduce Nick. The Confidant: Nick usually confides how hereallyfeels about the Darlings and his frustrations with them to her.

characters.name: Kiki George
characters.description: Nick and Lisa's daughter. Morality Chain: Nick tries his best to be a moral and constant presence in Kiki's life due to knowing the suffering of Dutch prioritizing the Darlings over him. Satellite Family Member: She exists largely in relation to Nick and Lisa, rather than having independent storylines.

characters.name: Dutch George
characters.description: Nick's father. The previous family lawyer to the Darling family. The Ace: Dutch was highly effective and trusted as the Darling family’s fixer and lawyer, being known all over New York. Affectionate Nickname: Almost everyone close to him refers to him by his nickname of Dutch. Amoral Attorney: Unlike his son, he didn't have much scruples and routinely enabled and concealed the Darlings' illegal or unethical actions. Best Friend: Tripp and Dutch regarded each other as their closest friend and confidants.

characters.name: Tripp Darling
characters.description: The billionaire patriarch of the Darling family, whose wealth and power allow him to manipulate political, legal, and social systems to protect his children and maintain his legacy. Best Friend: Tripp and Dutch regarded each other as their closest friend and confidants. Control Freak: Tripp repeatedly goes to extreme lengths to "set a path" to his children, such as bribing a theater to hire Juliet as an actress despite her beingterribleat acting. Cool Old Guy: Tripp can be quite jovial and progressive for a rich old man. Notably, he's one of the characters who treats Carmelita (a trans woman) with the most respect, and while he's naturally against her having an affair with Patrick due to the optics, he never once doubts that the feelings they have for each other are real. Corrupt Corporate Executive:Downplayed. He actually tries to be anHonest Corporate Executive, but won't hesitate to resort to corruption and dirty dealings when he's under pressure by Simon.

characters.name: Letitia Darling
characters.description: Tripp's wife, mother of five children. She's a New York socialite with a lot of influence and many secrets. Affectionate Nickname: Tripp calls her "Tish" during intimate moments. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Letitia presents herself as warm, gracious, and maternal, but she can be very manipulative and two-faced, havinglied to both her lovers for most of her life, sabotaging Nick's relationship with Lisa for Karen's sake and encouraging her son to seduce Nola so she'd get exposed and fired. Clear My Name: In season 2, she's arrested for being a suspect in Dutch's death and half of the season revolves around Nick trying to clear her name. Doting Parent: She's constantly showering her children with gifts and emotional support, ignoring the danger of spoiling them.

characters.name: Patrick Darling
characters.description: The eldest Darling son. A successful New York State Attorney General turned up-and-coming politician with a troubled romantic life. Affectionate Nickname: His family calls him "Pat" a lot of the time. Butt-Monkey: Patrick's docile,Extreme Doormatnature means he's constantly bullied by the other characters for laughs. The Charmer: He's naturally charismatic and is a great public speaker, which is the key to his success as a politician. Crusading Lawyer: Despite expectations, he's actually quite principled and ethical, refusing to use his position as Attorney General for the family's benefit if it involves anything illegal.He expects Nick to dirty his hands for him a few times though.

characters.name: Karen Darling
characters.description: The eldest Darling daughter. Has a complicated romantic history with Nick and a pattern of failed marriages. Attempted Homewrecker: The fact that Nick is married to Lisa never stops Karen from blatantly flirting with him, causing Nick and Lisa to have a lot of fights during season 1. By season 2, she's one of the factors that lead to them divorcing. Brutal Honesty: Karen is a bold woman who doesn't care how people perceive her, so she just blurts out whatever's on her mind, even when it's inappropriate or hurtful, without regard for consequences. Childhood Friend Romance: Nick and Karen have been attracted to each other since they were children and were each othersFirst Love. She becomes aNew Old Flamefor Nick when he returns to her life. Childhood Marriage Promise: Nick and Karen were romantically involved at one point in time when they were younger (she lost her virginity to him). Nick proposed to Karen while holidaying in Italy when they were both 19. They never ended up getting married, which Nick & Karen both regret, because the latter always thought she would end up his wife.

characters.name: Brian Darling
characters.description: The middle child of the Darling family. A Reverend with a lot of dirty laundry and a bitter resentment of Nick. As the Good Book Says...: Brian frequently quotes scripture and invokes religious doctrine in a sarcastic manner to make a point. Belligerent Sexual Tension: Brian and Andrea are constantly at each other's throats, but the fact that they rile each other up so much is also why they're so attracted to each other. A few of their arguments end with aSlap-Slap-Kiss. Black Sheep: Brian is treated as a disappointment within the family, with Nick himself referring to him as the "Black Sheep". Crisis of Faith: He starts questioning if he wants to be a man of the cloth in the middle of season 1 after both his lover and wife abandon him due to his sinful behaviour. The situation is settled for him when the church kicks him out when they find out about said sinful behaviour. During season 2, he gets the position back, but is much more cynical about his role.

characters.name: Jeremy Darling
characters.description: The youngest Darling son and Juliet's twin brother. A reckless youth with a substance abuse problem that frequently lands him in trouble. Attempted Homewrecker: When he grows a crush on Lisa, he starts hitting on her with little regard for the fact that she's Nick's wife. This earns him a punch in the face when Nick finds out. Book Dumb: He's very uneducated and lacks common sense, but he's very charming and emotionally manipulative when he wants to be. Brainless Beauty: Jeremy is Sweet and kinda earnest, but with a short attention span and is veryBook Dumb. Celebrity is Overrated: He repeatedly talks about how being ultra-rich often brings him just as much angst as it does joy and sometimes wishes he could be a normal middle-aged New Yorker.

characters.name: Juliet Darling
characters.description: The youngest Darling daughter and Jeremy's twin sister. A self-absorbed girl with acting ambitions. A-Cup Angst: She's quite distraught at being called a "twig" due to her lack of curves. Attention Whore: She embodies the excesses of the Darling lifestyle before exiting the family spotlight. The Ditz: She is portrayed as unintelligent and superficialUpper-Class Twit. Drama Queen: She's extremely dramatic and reacts to the most minor problems as if they were huge issues, such as her entire friendship with herBest Friendbeing ruined because Natalie copied her hairstyleonce.

characters.name: Ellen Alexander Darling
characters.description: Patrick's wife, who at first tries to deal with the Carmelita situation in a relatively reasonable manner, then loses it entirely. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Ellen presents as refined while being cruel and vindictive. Clingy Jealous Girl: She becomes obsessively focused on Patrick’s infidelity. Drowning My Sorrows: She increasingly turns to alcohol to deal with the stress of being cheated on. By season 2, she's a bona fideLady Drunk. Death by Falling Over:She dies while attacking Patrick in the shower while drunk due to tripping on the wet floor and hitting her head.

characters.name: Mei Ling Hwa Darling
characters.description: Brian's wife who divorces when she finds out he has an illegitimate child. The Bus Came Back: She's never seen again in season 1 after leaving Brian, but returns for one episode of season 2 for someSex with the Exwith Brian. Nice Girl: She gives the impression of being a morally upstanding and well-behaved woman who married a reverend out of the expectation he'd be the same. The moment she discovers how sinful he is, she promptly divorces him. Satellite Love Interest: Mei Ling primarily exists in relation to Brian and hardly interacts with any other character. Unwanted Spouse:Played With. Initially she was presented as a frigid ballbreaker, but her scenes with Brian (and his breakdown after she leaves him) shows that there isat the very leasta lot more mutual passion there than in any of the other examples. If anything this greater depth to their relationship might have backfired on the writers a little; more than a few fans have been frustrated that she wasPut on a Busso early.

characters.name: Andrea Smithson Darling
characters.description: Brian's second wife and the mother of his child. Belligerent Sexual Tension: Brian and Andrea are constantly at each other's throats, but the fact that they rile each other up so much is also why they're si attracted to each other. A few of their arguments end with aSlap-Slap-Kiss. Granola Girl: She's health-conscious, environmentally minded, and ideologically opposed to the excesses of the Darling lifestyle. She even wants to move out to Brazil to get away from the capitalist lifestyle of New York. Hates Rich People: She's not fond of the Darlings way of life and actually refuses to take Brian's money on several occasions. This is actually a huge part of why he likes her, since her distaste for riches means she likes Brian for who he is, not his money. Only Sane Woman: She's often the voice of reason in Brian's plotlines.

characters.name: Brian Darling Jr.
characters.description: Brian's illegitimate son. Adorably Precocious Child: He is unusually articulate and perceptive for his age. This leads to Brian reluctantly bonding with him. Cannot Tell a Lie: His attempts to pretend to be a Swedish orphandon'tgo well. Satellite Family Member: His role largely serves to motivate Brian’sCharacter Developmentrather than to carry storylines of his own.

characters.name: Simon Elder
characters.description: A wealthy and enigmatic businessman with a personal vendetta against the Darling family. Affably Evil: Simon is unfailingly charming, polite, and composed, even while taunting or bragging to Nick and the Darlings about his schemes. Arch-Enemy: He is the primary antagonist to both Tripp Darling personally and the Darling family as a whole due to their family history. Batman Gambit: Many of Simon’s schemes rely on others reacting exactly as he anticipates, often with multiple contingency layers, such as when he told Jeremy (whom Simon correctly suspected was faking amnesia) about Nola being aFemme Fatale, anticipating Jeremy would tell her and she'd realize he was testing Jeremy and would come to him, so he could now blackmail her into doing his bidding else he'd force Jeremy to do it. Big Bad: He's the main antagonist of the series, with his schemes being what Nick and the Darlings have to deal with for the majority of the two seasons.

characters.name: Carmelita Rainer
characters.description: Patrick's faithful lover. Betty and Veronica: She's the Betty in theLove Trianglebetween her, Patrick and Ellen, being the one who actually cares about Patrick's feelings and desires. The Confidant: Patrick frequently turns to her for emotional support and confesses to her the frustrations that come with being the firstborn Darling. Dropped a Bridge on Him:She's unceremoniously killed in the middle of season 2 when Chase's bullet accidentaly hits her. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: She willingly disappears from Patrick's life at the start of season 2 due to the belief that he may be better off with a mistress muddying up his life.

characters.name: Daisy
characters.description: Nick's secretary in his law firm. Beleaguered Assistant: Daisy is frequently overworked and dragged into Nick’s chaotic schedule once he starts working for the Darlings.Nick never considers hiring more people despite his and Daisy's workload increasing dramatically. Ditzy Secretary: It's aRunning Gagthat she often forgets to inform Nick that he has visitors in his office, which typically results in an embarrassing situation for him. Sassy Secretary: She can be quite snarky when talking to Nick, which he actually enjoys. ARunning Gaginvolves her setting him up with specificEmbarrassing Ringtonesfor each Darling family member.

characters.name: Natalie Kimpton
characters.description: Juliet's former best friend and Jeremy's girlfriend. The Baby Trap:Played With. When she first reveals to Jeremy that she's pregnant with his child, she actually believed it was true. But then when she found out shewasn't, she kept it a secret because of how excited he was over it. When he finds out, he's furious and assumes he was trying to fool him into marrying her, so he dumps her. Birds of a Feather: Natalie and Jeremy get along well due to both being rich and wild young adults who love partying and getting high. Crazy in the Head, Crazy in the Bed: She's a wild party girl who shows to be similarly wild in the bedroom and is implied to beverykinky in her intimate moments with Jeremy. Idle Rich: She's a rich young girl who does nothing with her life other than partying and chilling.

characters.name: Freddy Mason
characters.description: Karen's fourth fiancé. A second-rate golf player. Attention Whore: Part of why he likes being with Karen is due to loving the spotlight that it gives him by being part of the Darlings. Butt-Monkey: He's disrespected by everyone, the media thinks he's not good enough for Karen, the Darlings constantly mock him, and even Karen blatantly flirts with Nick in front of him, even as they're discussing their upcoming wedding. This is allPlayed for Laughs. Disposable Fiancée: He exists to be dumped by Karen on their wedding day. Gold Digger: He's mostly with Karen because of her family's money and doesn't really bother hiding it that much. This is why he can forgive her for cheating on him with his caddy without much issue.

characters.name: Sofia Montoya
characters.description: A middle-class woman who catches Jeremy's eye when he's working as a valet. Artists Are Attractive: She becomes much more interested in Jeremy after he starts pretending he's aStarving Artistand even wants him to do a nude painting of her. Buxom Beauty Standard: Her large breasts and cleavage are what first catch the attention of Jeremy and his valet friends. Head-Turning Beauty: Jeremy and the rest of the valets constantly ogle her whenever she shows up. Inter-Class Romance: Ultra-rich Jeremey Darling meets a beautiful woman named Sofia while temporarily working as a valet in one of his family's businesses. He falls in love with her, then pretends to be aStarving Artistto prove to himself he can win her over without disclosing his real identity as (essentially) anUpper-Class Twit. When he's inevitablyexposed, she promptly dumps him for having lied to her for so long.

characters.name: Sebastian Fleet
characters.description: Karen's third ex-husband. Amicable Exes: He has a good relationship with Karen despite being herthird ex-husband and has no issue signing the divorce papers when it comes up that they're still married. Hedoes'' seduce her for someSex with the Ex. Mr. Fanservice: He's a gorgeousHunkthat has only two scenes and is naked for both of them. Sex God: He's the best Karen's ever had, which is saying something since sheReally Gets Around. Shipper on Deck: He pushes Karen to pursue Nick, saying she'll never have a real relationship unless she gets him or gets over him.

characters.name: Nola Lyons
characters.description: A sharp and determined attorney who becomes deeply involved in legal conflicts surrounding the Darling family. Her motivations are shaped by both professional ambition and personal grievances. Big Sister Instinct: Her devotion to protecting her younger brother drives many of her most extreme and morally compromised decisions.This includes leaving Jeremy behind so she can raise her brother in a safer city. Deadpan Snarker: She frequently delivers dry, cutting remarks and sarcastic quips. The Determinator: She's a driven woman who pursues her goals relentlessly, becoming aHigh-Powered Career Womanwhen she was focused on her career, and becoming driven to set her brother free once Simon starts blackmailing her. She's even willing to kill and outrighttorturesSimon once she has the chance. Femme Fatale: She's an intelligent and seductive woman who uses her feminine wiles to manipulate others.

characters.name: Chase Alexander
characters.description: Ellen's brother, who becomes suspicious about the events surrounding her death. Amateur Sleuth: He knows no law enforcement in the world would investigate the Darlings, so he starts looking into his sister's death himself. Anti-Villain: While he's an antagonist towards Nick and the Darlings, Nick points out he's not entirely wrong in blaming them for Ellen's death, and while her death may have been an accident she caused, Tripp did have it covered up as her dying in a fire instead. Convenient Coma: After his shooting at Patrick's inauguration, he's shot and falls into a coma. An entire episode is dedicated to characters arguing over whether or not they should pull the plug on him, only for him to pass away in his sleep at the end. Grief Makes You Crazy: His personal issues with his family, combined with the loss of his sister drives him to the edge, which leads to him trying to shoot Patrick at his inauguration.
